TrueX · Post-grad Internship

Designing Rainbow
Trust from brand
to funded pitch.

A healthcare data privacy platform where patients own and control their entire medical history. I designed the brand identity and full UI and the CEO used it to secure investor interest.

⏱ 4 months 🎨 Brand Identity 📱 UI Design 👤 Solo Designer 👥 Team of 6–7 💰 $300k in funding ⭐ 87% satisfaction score
✦ what this internship delivered

My Figma file walked into an investor room. It walked out with $300k in funding.

$300k
raised by TrueX in investor funding, with my Figma designs as the centrepiece of the pitch
87%
customer satisfaction score achieved through design sprints and iterative feedback from real users
17
hi-fi Figma screens designed solo across two user types patients and hospital admins
👤 solo designer, full ownership
Brand identity, design system and every screen built from zero in 4 months.

As the only designer on a 6 to 7 person team, there was no senior designer to hand things off to. The colour system, typography, component library and all 17 screens were mine to own end to end.

💰 design that closed a funding round
The CEO used the Figma prototype in the investor pitch. Not a polished deck. The actual designs.

That's the difference between design as decoration and design as strategy. When investors are evaluating whether a product is real, what they're looking at is the UI. This one convinced them it was.

✦ why this project matters to me
I was the only designer on the team. Everything brand, UI, every screen came from me. And then my CEO walked into an investor room with my Figma file and raised $300k.

That's a level of responsibility I didn't expect from an internship. It taught me that design isn't decoration it's the thing that makes people believe in a product.

The Problem

healthcare data is broken ✦

Hospital software systems hold sensitive patient data but patients have no visibility, no control, and no easy way to share their own records. Every visit starts from scratch. Privacy is an afterthought.

TrueX was building Rainbow Trust to fix this a unified platform where each patient owns their entire medical history and can share it with a single click.

🏥
Fragmented records
Data scattered across hospitals, labs, clinics no single source of truth
🔒
Zero patient control
Patients can't see, edit or share their own health data
📋
Manual sharing
Sharing records with a new doctor means paperwork and phone calls
🚨
Emergency gaps
Critical info allergies, blood type inaccessible in emergencies

My Role

only designer on the team ✦
Brand Identity

Designed the complete brand for Rainbow Trust from zero colour system, typography, visual language, and a full stylesheet the engineering team used to build the product.

Full UI Design

Designed every screen in Figma login, onboarding, patient dashboard, medical history, immunization records, emergency contacts, sharing flow and document signing.

🏆 the funding moment
My CEO presented the Figma prototype to investors and raised an additional $300k in funding."

The interactive prototype wasn't just a demo it was the argument. Alongside running design sprints with Product and Engineering that achieved an 87% customer satisfaction score, stakeholders gave concrete inputs: notary, digital signatures, and appointment booking. That feedback became the next sprint.

Funding raised
$300k ✓
Satisfaction score
87% ✓
Team
Solo designer of 6–7 ✓

What I Designed

every screen, every component ✦
01
Unified Medical History
All records, lab results, prescriptions and visit notes chronological, searchable, owned by the patient.
02
One-Click Sharing
Share records securely with any new doctor controlled, revocable, instant.
03
Emergency Access
Emergency contacts, blood type, allergies critical info available when it matters most.
04
Immunization Records
Full vaccination history with dates, providers and certificates digital and always accessible.
05
Appointment Booking ✦
Book directly through the platform. Added after investor feedback during the pitch.
06
Digital Signature + Notary ✦
Legally validate documents with e-signature and notary. Another direct stakeholder input.

✦ features added directly from investor feedback

Brand Identity

stylesheet + visual system ✦

Clean, minimal, clinical designed to feel trustworthy and calm. Every colour, font and component was intentional. This stylesheet was the single source of truth for the engineering team.

✦ colour palette
#11573F
Dark Green Primary
#5EF38C
Bright Green Accent
#103E64
Dark Navy Secondary
#CCEEEE
Light Teal Background
Dark Green
Trust, nature, health. All primary CTAs and key UI elements.
Bright Green
Energy and vitality. Used for success states and highlights.
Dark Navy
Authority and data. Secondary buttons and informational UI.
Light Teal
Calm and clinical. Background surfaces and subtle containers.

Manjari elegant, modern, multilingual-ready

✦ button components
Back
Share
confirm with OTP
Sign Me Up

next →
send verification
request
fill with smart autofill
Log In

Pill-shaped buttons throughout dark green for primary actions, navy for secondary, white outlined for tertiary. Consistent rounded language reflects the platform's approachable, human tone.

Final Screens

17 hi-fi Figma screens ✦
🔐 Log In
🏠 Patient Dashboard
🔔 Appointment Reminder
👥 Relationships & Contacts
🗂 Data & Storage Profiles
📋 Data & Storage Expanded View
📥 Received Requests
📤 Notarize Upload Documents
📨 Notarize Send & Verify
✍️ Digital Signature
📹 Connect Video Consultation
✦ dual interface not just patients

Hospital Admin Side

Rainbow Trust has two user types. I designed the full hospital-facing admin interface too dashboards, custom form builder, verification requests and settings.

🏥 Hospital Admin Dashboard
⚙️ Settings
📤 Sent Requests
📋 Custom Form Designing
🛠 New Form Builder
💾 Form Preview & Save
✦ what I took away

When your CEO walks into an investor room with your Figma prototype and walks out with $300k design stops being a deliverable. It becomes the argument.

Being the only designer taught me to think in systems. Brand and UI can't be separate decisions every colour in the stylesheet is also a UX decision. Designing for healthcare means earning trust with every pixel.

The stakeholder inputs notary, digital signatures, appointment booking were the most valuable brief I've ever received. Real users, real stakes, real feedback that shaped the next version.

That's all the case studies
